SEARCH
Package app.service.impl.anomalies

Class QuantumAnomaliesDetectionService

java.lang.Object
app.service.impl.anomalies.QuantumAnomaliesDetectionService
All Implemented Interfaces:
AnomaliesDetectionService
public class QuantumAnomaliesDetectionService extends Object implements AnomaliesDetectionService
Quantum-inspired anomaly detection service.

The public API is intentionally identical to the classical implementation: trainIsolationForest(List) still receives historical DataRecord values and returns a Smile IsolationForest; searchForAnomaly(IsolationForest, List, double) still receives that model, the target records and the UI threshold.

The difference is the representation used internally. Before fitting/scoring, each OHLCV row is encoded into a simulated 5-qubit quantum feature map:

  1. Open, High, Low, Close and Volume are standardized against the training distribution.
  2. Each standardized feature is angle-encoded into a qubit rotation.
  3. A small variational-like circuit is simulated with Ry rotations, controlled-phase entanglement and a second mixing layer.
  4. The 32 computational-basis measurement probabilities are used as the feature vector for Isolation Forest.

This is a local simulator, not a call to quantum hardware. It is useful when the project must stay dependency-free and compatible with the existing controller/factory, while still using quantum-style angle encoding, superposition, entanglement phases and interference before the isolation step.

  • Method Details

    • trainIsolationForest

      public smile.anomaly.IsolationForest trainIsolationForest(List<DataRecord> data)
      Trains an Isolation Forest over simulated quantum measurement probabilities instead of raw OHLCV values.
      Specified by:
      trainIsolationForest in interface AnomaliesDetectionService
      Parameters:
      data - historical records used as normal baseline
      Returns:
      trained IsolationForest over quantum-encoded records

    • searchForAnomaly

      public List<AnomalyResult> searchForAnomaly(smile.anomaly.IsolationForest isolationForest, List<DataRecord> data, double threshold)
      Scores target records through the same quantum feature map used during training.

      The method returns one AnomalyResult per input record, preserving the current GUI behavior where the threshold is drawn as a chart marker and the full score series is displayed.

      Specified by:
      searchForAnomaly in interface AnomaliesDetectionService
      Parameters:
      isolationForest - model returned by trainIsolationForest(List)
      data - target records to score
      threshold - UI threshold; validated to keep API semantics, but not used to remove chart points
      Returns:
      scored records with feature contribution percentages
